name: Magnesium (as Bisglycinate)
historicalUse: Magnesium-rich mineral waters and spa therapies have been used for cardiovascular and musculoskeletal conditions since Roman antiquity. The mineral itself was formally isolated in 1808, but magnesium-rich natural environments have been used therapeutically across history. Arabic physicians recommended mineral-rich spring waters and specific foods high in what we now know to be magnesium content for cardiac palpitations and muscular cramping.
classicalSources: Ibn Sina — mineral therapeutics in Canon of Medicine
modernEvidence: Magnesium is an essential cofactor for over 300 enzymatic reactions including ATP synthesis, DNA repair, and muscle contraction. Deficiency is associated with hypertension, arrhythmia, insulin resistance, and increased cardiovascular risk. Bisglycinate chelate (Albion® TRAACS™) offers superior bioavailability and GI tolerability compared to oxide, citrate, or sulfate forms. Clinical studies support magnesium supplementation for blood pressure reduction, arrhythmia prevention, and migraine prophylaxis.
mechanisms: Calcium channel antagonism — competes with calcium for vascular smooth muscle binding sites, promoting vasodilation
Na/K-ATPase cofactor — maintains cellular electrolyte balance essential for cardiac rhythm
Insulin sensitization — GLUT4 translocation and insulin receptor signaling require magnesium
NMDA receptor modulation — supports neurological calm and sleep quality
benefits: Supports normal blood pressure already within healthy range
Promotes muscular relaxation and sleep quality
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile at dietary supplement doses. Albion bisglycinate minimizes laxative effect common with oxide and sulfate forms. Renal clearance is efficient in healthy individuals.
contraindications: Renal impairment — reduced clearance; use with caution and physician oversight in CKD
Hypermagnesemia — monitor in patients with renal disease
drugInteractions: Antibiotics (tetracyclines, fluoroquinolones) — magnesium reduces absorption; separate by 2+ hours
Diuretics — thiazides reduce magnesium excretion (beneficial); loop diuretics increase magnesium loss
Diabetes medications — monitor glucose due to insulin-sensitizing effect
physicianNotes: Bisglycinate form is the preferred clinical choice — superior absorption, minimal laxative effect, and excellent tolerability. Particularly valuable in patients with hypertension, type 2 diabetes, or metabolic syndrome. Monitor serum magnesium in patients with CKD. Therapeutic range: 300–400mg elemental magnesium daily.
patientGuidance: Magnesium bisglycinate is a gentle, well-absorbed form. If you take antibiotics, separate your doses by at least 2 hours. If you have kidney disease, consult your physician before use.
